creator: glebius@FreeBSD.org
text:
After some thinking on the problem, I'm closing this bug report.  We can't provide workarounds for every broken application, especially if it is broken in such a straightforward way. At least on the kernel side. You are welcome to write a shim library to LD_PRELOAD it for musl that would clear MSG_DONTWAIT.

I probably have speculative explanation why it works on Linux. Not because it is faster. I suppose Linux processed the request synchronously in the context of the send(2) syscall. So once the send(2) returns, there already exist data in the socket that can be received.
